data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent SC "Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a AC1 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip of comments/links/parent through the production path, no live Jira) + AC2 (every NOT-SYNCED/"as expected" probe guard converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes / removed; none silently passes on a defect) + AC3 (hermetic round-trip in CI; documented opt-in live variant for the real-Jira directions). FULLY.
- Parent AC "Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path" → 822a AC1. FULLY.
- Parent AC "Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" → 822a AC2 (audit scripts/jira-pressure-test/*.sh; convert every NOT-SYNCED/"as expected" guard to xfail/fail-when-the-gap-closes or remove). FULLY.

CONTRIBUTED:
- Parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" → 822a AC4 (assertions check round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality).
- Parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" → in-CI hermetic round-trip (AC3) staying green.

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT item (producer↔consumer schema/contract → story B/8937) is consistent with the roster and with 822a's declared depends_on fe57 for the fixtures/fake:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" + AC "fetcher snapshot validated vs schema" + AC "each differ's reads validated vs schema" + AC "one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures" + SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape" → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake fidelity).
- AC "outbound collapsed to a single production path" + AC "retired outbound applier path deleted" + SC "outbound exercised through the production path, not a dead path" → 33d0.
- AC "outbound sub-op telemetry surfaced" + AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly (warn-first)" + SC "silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary" → 2359.
- AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" → 8937 (story B): description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact … commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail … a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it" + 8937 AC5. The parent AC NAMES its owner verbatim; under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts) this is FULLY covered.

ANTI-FP — the earlier "Parent AC6 documented anti-change-detector guardrail only PARTIALLY covered" finding filed on this ticket's first two G3 audits is a FALSE POSITIVE and stays WITHDRAWN. It was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text; the actual parent AC explicitly names story B (= 8937) as owner and is delivered by 8937's description + AC5. Already withdrawn on this ticket's later audits and across siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57; not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY (OWNED by THIS child) — parent SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected"; parent AC "hermetic bidirectional round-trip"; parent AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded". CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector" (822a AC4) + parent AC "suite green" (in-CI round-trip). COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — schema/contract/round-trip (8937+fe57+59ef), applier-collapse + retired-path deletion (33d0), telemetry/canary (2359), documented guardrail (8937 AC5, named owner). STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; full reconciler suite green". UNCOVERED — none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion, and this child fully delivers its owned share (SC4/AC9/AC10: the hermetic bidirectional round-trip and the probe de-encoding audit).
